Faq.title: The essentials, briefly
Faq.lede: We answer what worries buyers from abroad most.
Faq.q1: How much does it cost to buy an apartment in Portugal?
Faq.a1: Beyond the property price there are one-off taxes (IMT, stamp duty) and services (notary, registration, lawyer). We calculate it all upfront — no surprises.
Faq.q2: Can a non-resident get a mortgage?
Faq.a2: Yes. Portuguese banks lend to non-residents usually up to 60–70% of the value for up to 30 years. You can estimate the payment in our calculator above.
Faq.q3: Do you need a visa to buy? What are D7 and D8?
Faq.a3: You don't need a visa to buy property. To relocate, people use D7 (passive income) and D8 (remote work). Visas are handled by our partner lawyers — we connect and coordinate.
Faq.q4: Is there a Golden Visa for buying property?
Faq.a4: No. Since 2023 the Golden Visa for real estate has been abolished. We honestly guide you by the current 2026 rules, not outdated promises.
Faq.q5: How do payments and transfers from Russia work?
Faq.a5: We help navigate payment and proof of funds under bank requirements (KYC). We review your case individually on a consultation.

Journal.title: From the Journal: analysis with numbers
Journal.lede: The essentials for a Porto buyer in brief — taxes, investing and relocation. Full guides in the journal.
Journal.whyNow: Why it matters now: the Porto market is up double digits, the under-35 IMT Jovem relief genuinely saves tens of thousands of euros, and the property golden visa is closed. We break it all down honestly, with 2026 numbers.
Journal.a1_summary: Every tax you'll pay when buying and owning property in Portugal in 2026 — IMT, stamp duty, IMI, AIMI. Plus the September rule change for non-residents.
Journal.a2_summary: International schools in Porto cost €4,860–22,736 a year — a fraction of London fees. British, IB, French and German options compared, plus where to live.
Journal.a3_summary: €3.9bn of foreign investment, 28% of all transactions. The data behind Portugal's property market in 2026 — real yields, entry prices, and the honest caveats.
Journal.hero_title: Guides and Deep-Dives on Porto Property
Journal.hero_lede: Honest and with 2026 numbers: taxes, investing, relocation, mortgages and life in Porto. Everything you need to know before buying an apartment in Portugal.
Journal.a1_excerpt: Every tax you'll pay when buying and owning property in Portugal in 2026 — IMT, stamp duty, IMI, AIMI. Plus the September rule change for non-residents.
Journal.a2_excerpt: International schools in Porto cost €4,860–22,736 a year — a fraction of London fees. British, IB, French and German options compared, plus where to live.
Journal.a3_excerpt: €3.9bn of foreign investment, 28% of all transactions. The data behind Portugal's property market in 2026 — real yields, entry prices, and the honest caveats.
Journal.a4_excerpt: What Porto actually costs in 2026 — rent, groceries, schools, healthcare, transport. Honest monthly budgets for singles, couples and families.
Journal.a5_excerpt: D7 (passive income, from €920/month) or D8 (remote work, from €3,680/month)? 2026 requirements, timelines, common mistakes — and how housing fits in.
Journal.a6_excerpt: How buyers save €10,000–30,000 on a Portuguese property purchase: negotiation, tax timing, mortgage shopping, currency transfers. The 2026 playbook.
Journal.a7_excerpt: From signing the CPCV without a lawyer to buying an “Airbnb apartment” you can't legally let — 8 real mistakes foreign buyers make in Portugal, priced.
Journal.a8_excerpt: Renting a two-bed in Porto costs €1,000+/month; the mortgage on the same flat runs from €750. When buying beats renting — a worked 2026 example.
Journal.a9_excerpt: Planning an Airbnb investment in Porto? New short-term rental licences aren't issued in the centre. Where you still can, what it really nets, and the smarter play.
Journal.a10_title: Buying in Porto from the UK or US
Journal.a10_excerpt: How UK and US buyers purchase Porto property remotely in 2026: currency transfers, source-of-funds checks, power of attorney, taxes — step by step.

Privacy.meta_description: How Avento processes and protects your personal data.
Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.disclaimer: ⚠️ This is a prototype draft. Before launch the text must be reviewed by a lawyer, and the details (legal entity, AMI licence number, contacts) replaced with real ones.
Privacy.intro: Avento respects your privacy. This page explains what data we collect through the site, why, and how you can control it.
Privacy.s_data_body: Only what you provide in the forms: name and phone (enquiry), email (newsletter), plus anonymised analytics — and only after you consent to cookies.
Privacy.s_use_body: To contact you about your request, match properties, and — if you subscribed — send news about Porto real estate. We do not sell your data.
Privacy.s_basis_body: Processing of data from the forms is based on your explicit consent (the checkbox when you submit an enquiry). You can withdraw consent at any time.
Privacy.s_retention_body: We keep enquiry data for as long as needed to handle your request, then delete or anonymise it. Exact periods will be finalised before launch.
Privacy.s_rights_body: You have the right to access, correct or delete your data, and to withdraw consent. Contact us via the details below.
Privacy.s_cookies_body: Analytics cookies (e.g. Google Analytics) load only after you click “Accept” in the consent banner. Until then, no non-essential cookies are set.
Privacy.s_contact_body: Questions about your data — via our contacts (WhatsApp / Telegram / email — placeholders). The company and AMI licence number will be listed here before launch.

About.meta_title: About — the Avento Team in Porto
About.meta_description: Avento is a Porto real estate agency with an office and an AMI licence. On the buyer’s side: transparent, honest, end-to-end, including remotely.
About.hero_title: A Team You Can Trust With a Purchase Abroad
About.hero_lede: Avento is a Porto real estate agency with a real office and an AMI licence. We’re on the buyer’s side: we source, vet and run the deal end-to-end — transparently and honestly, including fully remotely.
About.teamphoto_hint: A real photo of the team or the Porto office will replace this placeholder
About.approach_title: Why We Do Real Estate Differently
About.approach_p1: Buying an apartment in another country comes down to trust. Too often a foreigner can’t tell what they’re paying for, who’s on their side, or whether they’re overpaying simply for being “not local”. We built Avento as the opposite of that.
About.approach_p2: We work on the buyer’s side: we show real numbers, explain every tax and step of the deal, and take no commission from you — the seller pays it. We focus only on Porto and its region — living knowledge of the districts, developers and prices. The team is physically here: visit the office or jump on a video call anytime. And we’re honest about the 2026 rules — no outdated promises like a “golden visa for an apartment”. Only what actually works today.
About.values_lede: Four principles we work by with every client — and that you can hold us to.
About.val_transp_body: Open terms, honest numbers and real calculations. No hidden fees or surprises at closing.
About.val_buyer_body: We protect your interest, not the seller’s. The seller pays the commission — for you we’re free.
About.val_porto_body: Hyper-local expertise: districts, developers, real prices. We know the city, not just the listings.
About.val_honesty_body: We guide by the current 2026 rules, with no false promises. We tell it straight — even when the answer is “no”.

About.member_rental_body: Letting and management after purchase: tenants, repairs, reporting.
About.trust_body: A real office and team on the ground, with an AMI licence. The purchase can be done fully remotely — we’ll show the apartment live online and close by power of attorney.

Taxes.meta_title: Taxes & Costs of Buying an Apartment in Portugal (2026)
Taxes.meta_description: What it really costs to buy in Portugal: IMT, stamp duty, notary, the under-35 relief. A 2026 cost calculator — honest, down to the euro.
Taxes.hero_title: What It Really Costs to Buy an Apartment in Portugal — Down to the Last Euro
Taxes.hero_lede: We break down every tax and fee on a purchase: what it is, who pays and exactly how much. We count it all up front — no surprises at signing.
Taxes.quick_2_l: Stamp duty on the purchase price
Taxes.quick_3_l: Buyer's commission — paid by the seller
Taxes.costs_title: What You Pay Once — at Purchase
Taxes.costs_lede: On top of the apartment price, a purchase brings taxes and fees. Here's what your entry cost is made of — and who pays what.
Taxes.cost_imt_desc: Imposto Municipal sobre Transmissões. The main purchase tax. The rate is progressive — it depends on the price and the purpose (own home or investment).
Taxes.cost_selo_desc: Imposto do Selo. A fixed levy on the deal amount, paid together with IMT before signing at the notary.
Taxes.cost_notary_desc: Escritura + Registo Predial. Executing and registering the title (the Casa Pronta service or a private notary).
Taxes.cost_lawyer_desc: Independent legal due diligence on the property and the deal. Recommended for remote purchases and non-residents.
Taxes.cost_avento_desc: For the buyer our service is free — the seller pays the commission. We're on your side, with no hidden add-ons.
Taxes.calc_title: Calculate the Taxes and Costs on a Purchase
Taxes.calc_lede: Pick your situation and the apartment price — we'll show the one-off taxes and fees and your total entry cost.
Taxes.res_costs_label: One-off costs on top of the price
Taxes.disclaimer: An estimate for 2026. IMT is charged on the higher of the deal price or the tax value (VPT). The notary confirms the exact figure. Not tax advice.
Taxes.note_full: ✓ First home up to €330,539 — full exemption from IMT and stamp duty.
Taxes.note_partial: Partial relief: exempt up to €330,539; IMT of 8% on the excess.
Taxes.note_over: Above €660,982 the IMT Jovem relief no longer applies — calculated as a permanent home.
Taxes.note_nr: Purchase as investment / second home. Full IMT rate, no youth relief.
Taxes.jovem_lede: Since 2024 Portugal has run IMT Jovem: young buyers of a first own home are fully exempt from IMT and stamp duty on amounts up to €330,539. It's open to foreigners too — provided you become a Portuguese tax resident.
Taxes.jovem_req_1: Aged 35 or under on the deal date
Taxes.jovem_req_2: A Portuguese tax resident — or become one within 60 days of the deal (any citizenship)
Taxes.jovem_req_3: First purchase of an own permanent home
Taxes.jovem_req_4: Haven't owned a home in the last 3 years
Taxes.jovem_req_5: Not counted as a dependent for IRS purposes
Taxes.jovem_example: Example: a €300,000 apartment → around €12,900 saved (IMT + stamp duty) versus a regular purchase.
Taxes.annual_lede: Once the apartment is yours, one main annual tax remains. The others apply only to some owners.
Taxes.an_imi_desc: On the tax value (VPT), not the market price. The rate is set by the Porto municipality. Usually a few hundred euros a year.
Taxes.an_aimi_desc: Applies only if the combined tax value of your Portuguese property exceeds €600,000 per person. It doesn't affect most buyers.
Taxes.an_rent_desc: If you let the apartment, the income is taxed. Long-term lets get reduced rates. The exact figure depends on the rental type and your status — we'll work it out individually.
Taxes.mv_lede: It's useful to know in advance what happens on a sale. In brief — we'll work out the details for your situation.
Taxes.mv_res_desc: 50% of the gain is taxed on the progressive IRS scale. There's an exemption if the proceeds are reinvested in a new main home in Portugal.
Taxes.mv_nr_desc: The gain on sale is taxed; EU/EEA residents may use the resident regime (50% of the gain). Rates and reliefs depend on your country and double-taxation treaties.
Taxes.mv_reduce_desc: Purchase costs (including IMT and stamp duty), documented improvements and sale costs are deducted from the gain. That's why it's important to keep every receipt.
Taxes.faq_title: Taxes — Short and to the Point
Taxes.faq_q1: Is IMT charged on the price or the tax value?
Taxes.faq_a1: IMT and stamp duty are charged on the higher of two figures: the deal price or the property's tax value (VPT). The purchase price is usually higher than the VPT, so that's what's used — as in our calculator.
Taxes.faq_q2: When and how are the purchase taxes paid?
Taxes.faq_a2: IMT and stamp duty are paid before signing the deed at the notary — it's a condition of the deal. We prepare and file everything in advance so the signing day runs without delays, including remotely by power of attorney.
Taxes.faq_q3: Do I need a NIF to buy and pay taxes?
Taxes.faq_a3: Yes. A NIF (Portuguese tax number) is required for any property deal and tax payment. Getting a NIF is one of the first steps we handle for you, including remotely.
Taxes.faq_a4: The seller pays the agency commission. For the buyer, Avento's service is free — we work on your side and take no hidden add-ons.
Taxes.faq_q5: Can I get the under-35 relief as a foreigner?
Taxes.faq_a5: Yes — citizenship doesn't matter. The key condition: become a Portuguese tax resident (or switch residency within 60 days of the deal) and buy a first own permanent home. It doesn't apply to a non-resident investor.
Taxes.faq_a6: The IMT scale is indexed annually — for 2026 the thresholds were raised by about 2%. We run our figures on the current 2026 Portal das Finanças data and update them as things change.
Taxes.lead_heading: We'll Cost Out Your Budget End to End
Taxes.lead_lede: Leave your details — we'll calculate the full purchase cost for your situation and tell you whether you qualify for the relief. Free and no obligation.

Investors.meta_title: Investing in Porto Property: Yields and Strategies
Investors.meta_description: 4 working Porto investment strategies with real numbers: rental, off-plan resale, own-instead-of-renting, renovation. Yields and full deal support.
Investors.hero_title: Investing in Porto Property: Yields and Strategies
Investors.hero_lede: Porto is one of Europe's fastest-growing markets. We break down 4 working strategies with real numbers: for income, for capital growth and for your own life.
Investors.quick_3_l: strategies for different goals and budgets
Investors.market_title: A Market Growing Faster Than Its Neighbours
Investors.market_intro: Portugal has posted one of the strongest growth rates in Europe — driven by a supply shortage, housing demand and foreign-buyer interest. Below is the trend by country and by Porto district.
Investors.country_note: Portugal combines high growth, a European legal system and clear liquidity. Dubai and Greece are sometimes stronger, but with more volatility.
Investors.porto_note: Porto and Foz are pricier and more liquid. Gaia, Gondomar, Valongo and Maia deliver stronger catch-up growth off a lower base.
Investors.sources: Sources: INE (House Price Index 2025), Idealista price reports. The 2026–2030 forecast is a scenario range, not an official forecast or a guarantee. Past growth doesn't guarantee future returns.
Investors.strat_title: Four Ways to Invest in Porto
Investors.strat_lede: From passive rental income to off-plan resale and renovation. Choose by goal and budget — and we'll run the numbers on your specific property.
Investors.sc1_desc: Rent covers the mortgage and gives cash flow, plus the property grows in value.
Investors.sc2_desc: Buy at an early stage, resell the right before you get the keys for more.
Investors.sc3_desc: Live in your own flat, pay less than rent and earn on its growth.
Investors.sc4_desc: Buy a property to renovate with bank financing and reduced VAT.
Investors.s1_title: Buy an Apartment and Let It
Investors.s1_desc: You put in ~30% of your own funds, the rest is a mortgage. Rent covers the payment and gives cash flow, while the property itself grows in value. New-build or resale — we work out which is better for your goal.
Investors.s1_sum: With ~€60,000 invested, the buyer controls a full-value asset: rent covers the mortgage, and pre-tax profit over 5 years is roughly €61,600–139,100, IRR 15.6–31.3% a year.
Investors.s1_disc: Calculated before taxes, IMT, stamp duty, notary, condomínio, insurance, vacancy, repairs, and bank and agency fees. Final taxes and costs are individual.
Investors.s2_desc: You buy an apartment in a new project at an early stage and resell the purchase right before getting the keys — at a more advanced stage the property is worth more. Your outlay is only the payments during construction.
Investors.s2_sum: On assignment the buyer gets back the €60,000 paid in and earns a premium for the price growth: €40,000–70,000 on top. Return on invested funds is 66.7–116.7% over 2 years.
Investors.s2_disc: An approximate calculation before taxes and fees. It depends on residency, holding period, CPCV terms, the developer's assignment permission, deal costs and fees. Not a guarantee of returns.
Investors.s3_title: Your Own Apartment Instead of Renting
Investors.s3_badge: For those living in Porto permanently
Investors.s3_desc: If you already rent in Porto, every month you pay someone else's mortgage. Buy your own flat and you pay less than rent, live in your own place and earn on its rising value.
Investors.s3_rent_sub: rent for a 3-room flat, €1,300–2,000 depending on the project
s3_rent_bullets: The money goes to the landlord
Investors.s3_buy_sub: mortgage: €200,000 property, €70,000 own funds, €130,000 loan at 4%
s3_buy_bullets: Payment lower than rent — for your own flat
The flat grows in value (≈ +€68,000 over 5 years at 6%/yr)
You live in your own place
Investors.s3_tip: 💡 With ~€70,000 invested, you swap €1,500 rent for a €620 payment — saving ~€880/mo (≈ €10,500/yr) and a growing asset instead of receipts.
Investors.s3_disc: An indicative example. The payment depends on the down payment, rate and term; rents and prices are Porto market benchmarks for 2026. The exact figure is worked out for your income and property.
Investors.s4_badge: Higher entry barrier — higher potential margin
Investors.s4_desc: Buying a property to restore with growth potential. The entry barrier is higher and experience is needed, but here bank financing of the works and government incentives apply — and we run the whole process.
Investors.s4_c1_d: Banks lend not only for buying the property to renovate but for the works themselves — the renovation money is part of the loan.
Investors.s4_c2_d: On restoration works in urban renewal zones (ARU) — reduced VAT instead of the standard 23%.
Investors.s4_c3_d: Rehabilitation programmes and a 30% IRS tax deduction on works costs (within a limit) in ARU zones.
Investors.s4_c4_d: Sourcing the property, estimating the budget, contractors, arranging incentives and taking it to sale or rental.
Investors.s4_note: Porto is within urban renewal zones (ARU), where a reduced 6% VAT on restoration works applies (confirmed in 2026) and tax incentives are available. Terms depend on the property address, ARU status and type of works — we check per project.
Investors.proc_title: We Calculate and Run the Deal Around Your Strategy
Investors.proc_lede: We don't sell 'square metres' — we source a property for your goal and calculate the return in advance, before the viewing, not after.
Investors.proc_1_d: We compute the yield, cash flow and taxes for a specific flat and your status.
Investors.proc_2_d: We find properties for the strategy, check them legally and arrange a remote deal.
Investors.proc_3_d: We run your file past the banks and help with ARU incentives and financing the works.
Investors.proc_4_d: After the purchase we let and manage: tenants, repairs, checking AL zones.
Investors.lead_heading: We'll Calculate the Yield for Your Budget
Investors.lead_lede: Leave your details — we'll come back with a strategy calculation and 2–3 properties for your goal. Free and no obligation.

Relocation.meta_title: Moving to Portugal: D7 and D8 Visas, Residence & PR (2026)
Relocation.meta_description: Two legal routes to Portugal without an EU passport — the D7 and D8 visas. Requirements, timelines, the path to citizenship and an honest take on the 2026 rules. We solve housing for the visa file.
Relocation.hero_title: Moving to Portugal: Where to Start
Relocation.hero_lede: The two main legal routes to living in Portugal for those without an EU passport — the D7 and D8 visas. We explain them honestly, with no 'golden visa for a flat' promises, and help with housing for the visa file.
Relocation.hero_warn: ⚠️ Buying property on its own does not grant residence — the 'golden visa' via housing has been closed since 2023. But your own address in Portugal is a meaningful part of the visa file for both D7 and D8. That's the part we solve.
Relocation.visa_title: D7 or D8: Which One Fits You
Relocation.visa_lede: Both visas lead to residence and, over time, to permanent residence and citizenship. The difference is the source of your income.
Relocation.d7_who: Retirees, rentiers, owners of let property, dividend recipients.
Relocation.d7_amount_note: passive income per applicant (+50% spouse, +30% child)
d7_reqs: Health insurance for the whole term
Income must be passive — a salary doesn't qualify
Relocation.d8_who: Remote employees and freelancers with contracts outside Portugal.
Relocation.d8_amount_note: remote-work income over the last 3 months
d8_reqs: A contract or verified freelance income
Porto is the 3rd most popular D8 destination
A 'working' profile is assessed more predictably by consulates
Relocation.visa_tip: 💡 A simple rule: income arrives without your effort (pension, rent, dividends) → D7. You earn it remotely → D8. Have both → usually D8.
Relocation.tl_title: From Visa to Citizenship — Step by Step
Relocation.tl_lede: Real timelines from application to the residence card — 4–9 months depending on the consulate and AIMA's workload.
Relocation.t1_d: Apply at the consulate in your country of residence; an entry visa.
Relocation.t2_d: You arrive and attend an appointment at the AIMA immigration service.
Relocation.t3_d: You receive your first residence permit card.
Relocation.t4_d: You renew the residence permit and live in the country.
Relocation.t5_d: After 5 years — the right to permanent residence. Citizenship — under the new 2026 rules, after 10 years.
Relocation.rules_lede: We guide you by the current rules, not by outdated promises. Here's what matters for 2026.
Relocation.r1_desc: Residence for buying housing has been closed since 2023. The investment route remains only via funds and other options.
Relocation.r2_desc: From 19 May 2026 the term rose from 5 to 10 years (7 years for EU/CPLP citizens). The count starts from the residence permit issue date.
Relocation.r3_desc: The right to permanent residence remains after 5 years of legal residence.
Relocation.r4_desc: Closed to new applicants — replaced by the narrower IFICI regime. We calculate taxes individually.
Relocation.role_title: We're Not a Visa Agency — and We Don't Pretend to Be
Relocation.role_lede: We honestly do our part and bring in trusted immigration-lawyer partners who handle the visa file itself.
Relocation.a1_d: We find a rental or a flat to buy — a strong argument for D7/D8.
Relocation.a2_d: We introduce trusted immigration lawyers and coordinate the process.
Relocation.a3_d: NIF, bank account, taxes — we handle it, including remotely.
Relocation.role_cta_d: Tell us about your situation — we'll suggest which visa is closer, solve the housing question and introduce you to a lawyer.
Relocation.lead_heading: Planning a Move on D7 or D8?
Relocation.lead_lede: Leave your details — we'll explain how to solve the housing question for the visa and introduce you to a lawyer partner. Free and no obligation.
Relocation.disclaimer: Requirements are stated for 2026; consulates and AIMA update the rules — check before applying. This information is for reference only and is not legal advice. Visas are handled by immigration-lawyer partners.
